pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 12 (TASK_DIR="/home/ga/zombie-debug"), export_result.sh line 11 (PROJECT_DIR="/home/ga/zombie-debug")
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 73 shows 'job-worker:' as docker-compose service definition
Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 75 ('container_name: job-worker' in docker-compose.yml), export_result.sh line 10 (CONTAINER_NAME="job-worker")

Code analysis: verifier.py line 71 implements 'elif zombie_count <= 2:' for passing criterion. verifier.py line 9 documents 'Zombie process count must be low (<= 2) (30 pts)'. This threshold is hardcoded in the verifier logic and is the actual criterion for task success.

privileged_info_summary: The task is a synthetic Docker debugging exercise creating a job-worker service container at /home/ga/zombie-debug. The task requires users to add init: true to docker-compose.yml to enable Tini as the init process. The actual zombie process count threshold for task success is <= 2 processes (as coded in verifier.py line 71), not the claimed threshold of 3. All file paths and service/container names in metadata are correct.
